steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: Before the blank: an independent clause with an appositive phrase ("the site with the highest elevation"). After the blank: an independent clause with subject "it" and verb "had shifted."

steps.title: Apply the comma-conjunction rule
steps.content: The rule is: a comma + coordinating conjunction properly joins two independent clauses. Choice D "elevation, and" correctly closes the appositive with a comma, then uses "and" to join the second clause. The other choices fail because they either lack the conjunction entirely (creating a boundary error) or embed it improperly within the appositive. That matches choice D — D is correct.
